text: 10g of fat. omega 3 is exactly the kind of fat support you want from a meat. for meats, fat quality matters a lot once the protein is already there.
0g of carbs. the rest is not doing much. in this category, carbs barely matter.
29g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. strong protein is expected here, so the rest of the profile decides how high it climbs.
vitamin b12 at 150% daily value, vitamin d at 40% daily value stand out most. that is major vitamin support.
iron at 25% daily value, zinc at 12% daily value stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

on the plus side: high satiety protein source because that is a major real-world strength for meat. complete protein delivery is reliable because the category is supposed to do this well. easy to centre a meal around because practical meal value matters.

the drawbacks next: fat quality can go sideways fast because some meats are strong until the fat profile shows up. cost and storage can be a pain because practical downsides still count. quality swings a lot by cut and processing because the category can look very different depending on what version you actually buy.
A tier. Best when you want efficient protein and can accept the category tradeoffs that come with it.
